emergency.seekCare: Seek immediate medical care for injuries.

footer.disclaimer1: AccidentPath is not a law firm and does not provide legal advice. Information provided is for educational purposes only. By using this site, you acknowledge that no attorney-client relationship is formed. If you are in immediate danger, call 911. For medical emergencies, seek care immediately.
footer.disclaimer2: AccidentPath connects consumers with attorneys in our network. Attorneys in our network may pay a fee for marketing services. This does not affect the quality of service you receive. AccidentPath does not endorse or guarantee any attorney's services. Availability varies by state and case type.
footer.disclaimer3: Every case is different. Consult a licensed attorney for advice specific to your situation. Results may vary. Past results do not guarantee future outcomes.

tools.disclaimer: This information is for educational purposes only and does not constitute legal advice.

findHelp.heroDescription: Answer 9 quick questions and we'll show you what to do after your accident.

urgency.highMessage: Based on your answers, your situation may require immediate attention. Legal deadlines may be running.
urgency.moderateMessage: Your situation deserves timely attention. Speak with an attorney soon to protect your rights.
urgency.standardMessage: Your next steps are clear. Continue with the following recommendations at your own pace.

results.heading: Your Personalized Results
results.noResults: No results found. Please try again.
results.lawyerType: Type of Lawyer Who Can Help

intake.step1_question: What type of accident did you have?
intake.step1_desc: Select the option that best describes your situation.
intake.step2_desc: The date affects your legal timeline. California and Arizona both have a 2-year statute of limitations for most personal injury claims.
intake.step2_timeSensitiveMsg: More than 18 months have passed since your accident. You may be approaching your legal deadline. Consider speaking with an attorney as soon as possible. This is educational information only, not legal advice.
intake.step3_desc: We currently serve California and Arizona. State laws affect your options.
intake.step4_desc: Select all that apply. If unsure, select your best guess.
intake.step5_desc: Medical documentation is important evidence. Select the option that best applies.
intake.step6_desc: Police reports are an important piece of evidence in accident cases.
intake.step6_tipMsg: You may still be able to file a report. Many police departments accept delayed reports within 24–72 hours. Check with your local department for options. This is educational information only, not legal advice.
intake.step7_desc: This helps us understand what options may be available to you.
intake.step8_question: Has the accident affected your ability to work?
intake.step8_desc: Lost wages may be recoverable. Select the option that best describes your situation.
intake.step9_desc: Almost done. Please review the consent below, then optionally share your contact info so we can send your results.
intake.step9_contactOptional: Contact info (optional — to receive your results by email)
intake.step9_privacyNote: Your contact info is never sold. By submitting, you agree to our
intake.tcpaConsent: By submitting this form, I agree to receive communications by phone, text, and email regarding my inquiry. I understand this service is free and I am not obligated to hire any attorney.
intake.disclaimer: AccidentPath is not a law firm and does not provide legal advice. Information provided is for educational purposes only.
